name: argon2-cffi (Argon2 Password Hashing for Python)

performance.notes: Intentionally slow for security; time_cost/memory_cost/parallelism configurable

agent_readiness.notes: Argon2id password hashing — winner of Password Hashing Competition 2015. from argon2 import PasswordHasher. ph = PasswordHasher(time_cost=2, memory_cost=65536, parallelism=2). hash = ph.hash('secret'). ph.verify(hash, 'secret'). ph.check_needs_rehash(hash) for parameter upgrades. Argon2id by default (recommended hybrid). Raises VerifyMismatchError on wrong password. Raises VerificationError on corrupt hash. Auto-generated salt. CFFI-based C bindings. Type annotations. Excellent docs. Preferred over bcrypt and PBKDF2 for new projects.

gotchas: Intentionally slow — not for high-frequency hashing (expected behavior)
Memory-hard — memory_cost parameter affects server RAM requirements
Requires tuning for your hardware (benchmark time_cost/memory_cost)
Different from passlib's argon2 — argon2-cffi is preferred standalone choice
best_when: Password hashing for new Python web applications
Upgrading from bcrypt/PBKDF2 to stronger hashing
When check_needs_rehash() for parameter migration is needed
avoid_when: Resource-constrained environments where memory_cost cannot be tuned
When passlib is already in use (passlib supports argon2 too)
summary: argon2-cffi provides Argon2id password hashing — winner of the 2015 Password Hashing Competition. PasswordHasher().hash('secret'). ph.verify(hash, 'secret'). ph.check_needs_rehash(hash) for parameter upgrades. Auto-generated salt. CFFI C bindings. Excellent error handling and docs. MIT by Hynek Schlawack. Preferred over bcrypt/PBKDF2 for new Python projects — Argon2id is the current OWASP recommendation.
